title: Thirsty Machines maps estimated water use across major AI data centers
friendlyTitle: A new map estimates how much water AI data centers use
summary: Thirsty Machines published an interactive estimate of direct water use by major AI and data-center campuses, highlighting how cooling methods, local climate, and evaporative systems affect consumption. The site stresses that the estimates are modeled rather than audited because operators rarely disclose site-level water usage.
friendlySummary: Thirsty Machines shows estimated water use from large data centers and explains how different cooling methods change the numbers.
whyItMatters: AI infrastructure decisions increasingly affect local utilities, permitting, energy planning, and public trust. For businesses and communities, the useful question is not only how powerful a model is, but where and how its compute is cooled, powered, and governed.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI needs physical infrastructure. Communities and companies need clearer information about water and energy use before building more of it.
